steps.title: Identify the requested definition
steps.content: The student wants to explain what a quenched galaxy is. The relevant notes say quenching is when a galaxy loses star-forming gas, a galaxy that no longer forms stars is called quenched, and a quenched galaxy has entered the poststarburst phase.

steps.title: Eliminate incomplete options
steps.content: Choice A describes a starburst galaxy before quenching, not what a quenched galaxy is. Choice B says a quenched starburst enters the poststarburst phase but does not explain the key meaning. Choice D describes the process leading to quenching rather than defining the term. The choice that defines a quenched galaxy as one no longer forming stars is best. The answer is C.
